questions.question: Which hospital is first opened in India?
questions.explanation: The Madras general hospital is the first hospital opened in India. It was opened in 1664 in chennai.

questions.question: When was the first National Health Policy of India created?
questions.explanation: In India the first National Health Policy (NHP) was launched in 1983. It was created with the goals of establishing a system with primary-care facilities and a referral system. In 2002, the updated NHP focused on improving the practicality and reach of the system as well as incorporating private and public clinics into the health sphere.

questions.question: In which year was national rural health mission launched?
questions.explanation: The National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) was launched in 2005. It is an initiative undertaken by the government of India to address the health needs of under-served rural areas.

questions.question: In which year was the Integrated child development scheme launched?
questions.explanation: The Integrated child development scheme was launched in 1975. Its main purpose is to improve nutrition and health status in children.

questions.question: In which year was the medical council act was passed?
questions.explanation: The government of India passes the Indian Medical Central Council Act in 1970. It was passed to standardize Ayurvedic teaching institutions, their curriculum and their diplomas.
